CREATE TABLE test_table (id INT, value INT);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, value) VALUES (1, 10);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, value) VALUES (2, 20);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, value) VALUES (3, 30);
SELECT * FROM test_table WHERE value <> 10;
SELECT * FROM test_table WHERE value <> 20 AND id <> 2;
SELECT * FROM (SELECT id, value FROM test_table) AS subquery WHERE value <> 30;
CREATE TABLE second_table (id INT, value INT);
INSERT INTO second_table (id, value) VALUES (1, 10);
INSERT INTO second_table (id, value) VALUES (2, 15);
SELECT * FROM test_table JOIN second_table ON test_table.id <> second_table.id;
SELECT * FROM test_table WHERE id NOT IN (SELECT id FROM second_table WHERE value <> 15);
SELECT CASE WHEN value <> 10 THEN 'Not Equal' ELSE 'Equal' END AS comparison_result FROM test_table;
SELECT t1.id, t2.value FROM test_table t1, second_table t2 WHERE t1.value <> t2.value;
SELECT * FROM test_table WHERE (value <> 10 OR value <> 20) AND id <> 1;
CREATE TABLE third_table (id INT, value INT);
INSERT INTO third_table (id, value) VALUES (1, 5);
SELECT * FROM test_table t JOIN third_table tt ON t.value <> tt.value WHERE t.id <> 3;
SELECT * FROM (SELECT id, value FROM test_table WHERE value <> 20) AS subquery1 WHERE id <> (SELECT id FROM second_table WHERE value <> 15);
SELECT DISTINCT * FROM test_table WHERE value <> 20 ORDER BY id;
